title: Gente
goals: ¿Cómo expreso lo que disfruto hacer con mi familia y amigos?
Have/has got, adverbs of frequency, arrangements, time expressions.
Hablar de la familia y coordinar planes.

aspects.title: La oficina
aspects.summary: Mostrar tu espacio, hablar de posesión y coordinar un plan.

aspects.title: have got: afirmativo
aspects.summary: Tener algo o a alguien. have con I/you/we/they, has con he/she/it.

aspects.title: have got: preguntas
aspects.summary: Have you got…? Has she got…? y respuestas cortas.

aspects.title: La familia
aspects.summary: Hermanos, hijos, abuelos: describir a tu familia y a la gente.

aspects.title: Frecuencia y how often
aspects.summary: Con qué frecuencia hacés algo (how often, once/twice a week).

aspects.title: Coordinar planes
aspects.summary: Are you free? How about…? What time is good for you?

aspects.title: La hora y las preposiciones
aspects.summary: at con horas, on con días, in con meses.

atoms.text: Here we are. This is my office. There is a PC, a desk and a comfortable chair.
atoms.gloss: Acá estamos. Esta es mi oficina. Hay una PC, un escritorio y una silla cómoda.
atoms.speaker: colleague
atoms.ipa: hˈɪɹ wiː ɑːɹ ðɪs ɪz maɪ ˈɑːfɪs ðɛɹ ɪz ɐ pˌiːsˈiː ɐ dˈɛsk ænd ɐ kˈʌmftəbəl tʃˈɛɹ

source.note: El cómic dice "Have got your own office?", omitiendo el sujeto. Corregido a la forma correcta que enseña la unidad.

atoms.text: Have you got your own office?
atoms.speaker: mary
atoms.ipa: hæv juː ɡɑːt jʊɹ ˈoʊn ˈɑːfɪs

atoms.text: How often do you work there?
atoms.speaker: mary
atoms.ipa: hˌaʊ ˈɔfən dˈuː juː wˈɜːk ðˈɛɹ

atoms.text: I usually work there, but I work at home once a week.
atoms.gloss: Normalmente trabajo ahí, pero trabajo desde casa una vez por semana.
atoms.speaker: colleague
atoms.ipa: aɪ jˈuːʒuːəli wˈɜːk ðˈɛɹ bˌʌt aɪ wˈɜːk æt hˈoʊm wˈʌns ɐ wˈiːk

atoms.text: How about going to my office?
atoms.gloss: ¿Qué tal si vamos a mi oficina?
atoms.speaker: mary
alternatives: How about coming to my office?
Would you like to go to my office?
atoms.ipa: hˌaʊ ɐbˌaʊt ɡˌoʊɪŋ tə maɪ ˈɑːfɪs

atoms.title: La oficina propia
atoms.situation: Un colega le muestra a Mary su oficina y coordinan pasar a la de ella.
variants: How often do you work here?

atoms.text: She has got a big family.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː hɐz ɡɑːt ɐ bˈɪɡ fˈæmɪli

atoms.text: She goes to the cinema twice a week.
atoms.gloss: Va al cine dos veces por semana.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː ɡoʊz tə ðə sˈɪnəmə twˈaɪs ɐ wˈiːk

atoms.prompt: How often do you play tennis?
promptVariants: How often does she play tennis?
atoms.speaker: student

atoms.prompt: How often do you visit your family?
atoms.speaker: student

atoms.prompt: Have you got any brothers or sisters?
promptVariants: Have you got a big family?
replies: Yes, I have got two sisters.
atoms.speaker: student

atoms.prompt: Has your family got a car?
atoms.speaker: student

atoms.prompt: Would you like to go to the cinema?

How about going to the cinema?

atoms.prompt: Is there a restaurant near here?
promptVariants: Is there a cinema near here?

atoms.prompt: How often does she go to the cinema?
promptVariants: How often do they go out?

student
replies: Yes. What would you like to do?

atoms.text: What would you like to do?
atoms.speaker: student-m
atoms.ipa: wˌʌt wʊd juː lˈaɪk tə dˈuː

atoms.text: How about going to the cinema?
atoms.speaker: student
alternatives: Would you like to go to the cinema?
atoms.ipa: hˌaʊ ɐbˌaʊt ɡˌoʊɪŋ tə ðə sˈɪnəmə

atoms.text: What time is good for you?
atoms.speaker: student
atoms.ipa: wˌʌt tˈaɪm ɪz ɡˈʊd fɔːɹ juː

atoms.title: Coordinar una salida
atoms.situation: Dos amigos organizan ir al cine: proponen, eligen la película y acuerdan la hora.
variants: What time is good for you?

atoms.text: Bill has got a big family.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: bˈɪl hɐz ɡɑːt ɐ bˈɪɡ fˈæmɪli

atoms.text: He has got four brothers and one sister.
atoms.gloss: Tiene cuatro hermanos y una hermana.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: hiː hɐz ɡɑːt fˈoːɹ bɹˈʌðɚz ænd wˈʌn sˈɪstɚ

atoms.text: I visit my grandmother in January.
atoms.gloss: Visito a mi abuela en enero.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪ vˈɪzɪt maɪ ɡɹˈændmʌðɚɹ ɪn dʒˈænjuːˌɛɹi

atoms.text: We go on holiday in December.
atoms.gloss: Nos vamos de vacaciones en diciembre.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: wiː ɡˌoʊ ˌɔn hˈɑːlɪdˌeɪ ɪn dᵻsˈɛmbɚ

source.note: Refuerza have got negativo, aspecto flaco.

atoms.prompt: Describe someone's family, like Bill's. Use has got, family words, and their names.
atoms.modelAnswer: Bill has got a big family. His father is an actor and his name is Stellan. He has got four brothers: Alexander, Gustaf, Valter and Sam. He has got one sister and her name is Eija. They all live together in a big house. Bill often visits his family.
modelVariants: My friend has got a small family. She has got one brother and no sisters. Her mother is a teacher and her father is a doctor. They have got a dog. They usually have dinner together.
I have got a big family. I have got two sisters and one brother. My grandmother lives with us. We often visit our cousins on Sundays. My family is very friendly.

rubric.text: Usa palabras de familia (brother, sister, father…)
Describe a la familia en oraciones completas

atoms.speaker: narrator

atoms.prompt: Complete with have got or has got (affirmative, negative or interrogative).
Rewrite the sentence with the frequency adverb in brackets.

accept: They usually work at the cinema.

atoms.prompt: Correct the mistake in the sentence.

source.note: La narrativa NO está en el material (link ausente). Escrita con léxico de la U3, consistente con las respuestas oficiales: no le gusta la TV, juega action games una hora o dos, sí navega internet.

atoms.narrative: Hi. My name is Sofia. I have got a computer and a phone. I don’t like TV — I never watch it. But I love video games. I usually spend an hour or two playing action games every day. I have got many friends online. I also surf the net a lot: I read the news and I chat with my family. On Saturdays I go out with my friends.
atoms.speaker: narrator

questions.q: How much time does she spend playing action games?

source.section: Actividad de cierre — Composition My Life Chapter 3

atoms.prompt: COMPOSITION: MY LIFE: CHAPTER 3. Write about the members of your family. Describe them briefly.
atoms.modelAnswer: I have got a big family. My father is a teacher and my mother is a doctor. I have got two sisters and one brother. My brother is very friendly and he plays football. My sisters are students. We have got a dog too. My grandmother lives near us and we visit her once a week. My family is very important to me.
modelVariants: I have got a small family. I have not got any brothers or sisters. My mother works in an office and my father is a pilot. We have got two cats. We often have dinner together and we watch movies on Fridays.

rubric.text: Describe a los miembros (son estudiantes, es simpático…)
Escribe un párrafo coherente sobre la familia

atoms.speaker: examiner
